generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically retrieve and refresh all Event and Activity data from Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 on a fixed 60-second cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates a 60-second automated refresh cycle to ensure real-time situational awareness during live exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to manually pause the automatic 60-second data refresh cycle and shall resume the cycle upon explicit user command.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 permits manual pause to support briefing moments or display freeze requirements while ensuring the cycle resumes on demand.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a Multi-Document Interface (MDI) layout that permanently allocates the left two-thirds of the display area to a graphical timeline view and the right one-third to a data table view; this ratio shall not be user-configurable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 fixes the 2:1 layout proportion to ensure consistent projection-ready display across all exercise operations center deployments.

generated_requirements.text: Within the data table region, the system shall display sequential (order-dependent, non-time-bound) Events in the top one-quarter of the table pane and temporally relevant Events ordered by proximity to current exercise time in the bottom three-quarters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 defines the segmentation of the table region to allow controllers to distinguish between sequential and time-bound Events at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall color-code all displayed Events and Activities based on their variance from current exercise time, using thresholds and colors that strictly conform to the DHS color schema standard.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and BR-05 require DHS-compliant color-coded schedule variance indicators to enable instant visual triage of schedule adherence.

generated_requirements.text: All visual elements of the application — including backgrounds, text, highlights, borders, and status indicators — shall conform to the Department of Homeland Security color schema; no custom or ad hoc color choices are permitted without formal DHS approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and RC-01 mandate full DHS color schema compliance across every UI element to satisfy regulatory and standards constraints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a graphical timeline in the left two-thirds region, plotting Events and Activities as nodes positioned according to their scheduled exercise time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future-state description requires a graphical timeline region to provide visual representation of Events relative to exercise time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display two independent, simultaneously visible clocks: one showing current local (wall-clock) time and one showing current exercise time, each visually distinct from the other.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires dual-clock display to eliminate confusion in exercises where exercise time diverges from wall-clock time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a direct deep-link from each displayed Event or Activity to its corresponding record in the originating source application (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), enabling users to navigate to the source record for modification without leaving the display context.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 establishes that EEMDS is a display-only tool and all edits must occur in source applications, requiring seamless deep-link navigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display only MSEL Summary data in both the graphical timeline nodes and the data table rows; full MSEL detail records shall not be rendered within EEMDS.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 restricts the data scope to MSEL Summary data to keep the display concise and projection-readable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate every user at login and shall deny access to any user who is not pre-authorized by the system administrator; no guest or anonymous access modes shall be supported.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 and RC-02 mandate positive authentication and strict access denial for unauthorized users to meet DHS security policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support role-based access control (RBAC), distinguishing at minimum the roles of Exercise Controller, Exercise Planner, IT Administrator, and After-Action Review Analyst, with permissions scoped appropriately to each role.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple stakeholder roles with distinct capabilities require RBAC to enforce least-privilege access across the system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users (Exercise Planners and above) to define, modify, and save user-configurable time segments used to organize Events on the timeline, prior to or during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires configurable time segments per exercise to allow planners to structure the timeline display to match exercise design.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Venue, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates venue-based filtering to reduce cognitive load for controllers managing multi-venue exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support filtering of displayed Events and Activities by Key Events designation, independently or in combination with other active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 mandates key-event filtering to allow controllers to isolate high-priority Events during fast-moving exercise scenarios.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an on-demand unfiltered view of all Events in the exercise, accessible regardless of any currently active filters.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 requires a full exercise view to ensure controllers can always access the complete Event set without clearing filters manually.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture a history record for every change to Event or Activity data reflected in EEMDS (originating from source systems), including the field changed, previous value, new value, timestamp, and user identity associated with the source-system change.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 requires a complete audit trail of all data changes to support post-exercise after-action review and reconstruction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall make the history report of all Event and Activity data changes available for retrieval at any point during or after the exercise, accessible to authorized AAR Analyst and Administrator roles.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and the AAR Analyst stakeholder need require on-demand access to the history report throughout and after exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate actual-versus-assumed time variance charts for Events and Activities, displaying the quantitative difference between scheduled and actual execution times.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future-state description and in-scope items require variance charts to provide quantitative schedule adherence analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Sync Matrix 1.0 in read-only mode, consuming Event and Activity data through its existing data interface (API, database connection, or file export) without modifying any data in the source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and BR-06 require read-only integration with Sync Matrix 1.0 using its existing interface to avoid source-system modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with Exercise Management Tool 1.0 in read-only mode, consuming Event and Activity data through its existing data interface without modifying any data in the source application.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and BR-06 require read-only integration with EMT 1.0 using its existing interface to avoid source-system modification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall surface all Events and Activities from both Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 in a single unified display without requiring manual data entry by any user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 5 requires full consolidation of both source systems into one display to eliminate fragmented data access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable as a Windows-based client application on a standard PC within the exercise operations center, with no dependency on server-side rendering or cloud infrastructure for core display functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains deployment to a client PC environment, requiring a self-contained Windows application architecture.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to configure and modify display settings (e.g., time segment definitions, active filters, and layout preferences within permitted bounds) before and during exercise execution.
generated_requirements.rationale: The in-scope item 'display configuration and modification capabilities' requires user-accessible configuration controls for exercise planners.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall visually indicate to the user when the data refresh cycle is paused, including the elapsed time since the last successful refresh.
generated_requirements.rationale: Users must be aware of data staleness when the refresh is paused to avoid acting on outdated information during exercise execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clear, persistent indicator showing the timestamp of the most recent successful data refresh from each source system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transparency about data currency is essential for controllers to assess the reliability of the situational awareness display.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the user with a visible on-screen alert when a data refresh attempt from either source system fails, identifying which source system is unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dependencies D-01 and D-02 identify source-system unavailability as a risk; users must be informed immediately to avoid acting on stale data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete each 60-second data refresh cycle — including data retrieval from both source systems, processing, and display update — within 10 seconds of the scheduled refresh trigger, such that the display is never more than 70 seconds stale under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 1 requires data staleness never to exceed 60 seconds; a 10-second processing budget ensures the refresh completes within the cycle window.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render all display updates (timeline graph and data table) within 3 seconds of completing a data refresh, with no perceptible degradation in display responsiveness during the refresh cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: OC-02 requires that the 60-second refresh cycle produce no perceptible performance degradation, necessitating a fast render budget.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve 99% availability during normal business hours (0800–1800 local time, Monday through Friday) over the first six months of operation; planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled outside these hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 3 and OC-01 set a 99% availability target during business hours to ensure the system is ready for all exercise operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use font sizes, contrast ratios, and layout proportions sufficient to ensure that Event and Activity detail data in the data table is legible to 90% of viewers at a distance of 30 feet from a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 8 and TC-04 require 30-foot readability of detail data on a 10×10 projection screen for operations center use.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use font sizes, color contrast, and graphical element sizes sufficient to ensure that color-coded Event status indicators in the timeline are interpretable by 90% of viewers at a distance of 100 feet from a 10×10 projection screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 2 requires color-coded status to be interpretable at 100 feet to support shared situational awareness in large operations centers.

generated_requirements.text: 90% of new users shall be able to independently initiate an Event or Activity display within 90 minutes of first use, without formal training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 4 sets a measurable learnability target to minimize the learning curve for exercise controllers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce positive user authentication at every login attempt and shall correctly classify authorized versus unauthorized users in 100% of access attempts, with zero unauthorized access incidents.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Objective 7 and BR-08 require perfect access control enforcement to meet DHS security policy and protect exercise data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable DHS IT security policies governing user authentication, session management, and access control for internal operational tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: RC-02 mandates compliance with DHS IT security policies as a non-negotiable regulatory constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce session timeout after a configurable period of inactivity, requiring re-authentication before the display can be interacted with again.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS IT security policies and operational context require session controls to prevent unauthorized use of an unattended display terminal.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between EEMDS and the source systems (Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0) shall be protected in transit using encryption consistent with DHS IT security standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS IT security compliance requires that exercise data not be exposed in plaintext during integration data transfers.

generated_requirements.text: The history report and audit log shall be tamper-evident; any unauthorized modification to log records shall be detectable by the system or an administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: The integrity of the audit trail is critical for after-action review; tamper evidence ensures log records are trustworthy for post-exercise analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain history reports and audit logs for a minimum of 12 months after the conclusion of each exercise, or as otherwise required by DHS records retention policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: AAR Analysts and compliance officers require durable, long-term access to change history for after-action reporting and regulatory review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a source-system connection failure and automatically resume data refresh within one refresh cycle (60 seconds) of the source system becoming available again, without requiring manual intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dependencies D-01 and D-02 identify source-system instability as a risk; automatic recovery minimizes display downtime during transient failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall preserve the last successfully retrieved dataset and continue displaying it (with a staleness indicator) in the event of a source-system connection failure, rather than clearing the display.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clearing the display on a transient failure would eliminate situational awareness; preserving the last known state maintains operational utility.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source-system integration layer shall be designed with a documented, versioned data contract for each source system (Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0), such that changes to the integration interface can be isolated and updated without requiring changes to the display layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration fidelity is the highest-risk dependency; a decoupled integration layer reduces the cost and risk of adapting to source-system interface changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system's color schema configuration shall be externalized into a configurable settings file or administration panel, such that updates to DHS color schema thresholds can be applied without recompiling or redeploying the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS color schema thresholds may evolve; externalizing them reduces maintenance cost and eliminates the need for code changes on schema updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any standard Windows 10 or later client PC meeting the minimum hardware specifications defined in the deployment guide, without requiring custom hardware or specialized drivers.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 requires standard client PC deployment; broad Windows compatibility ensures the system can be set up in any DHS operations center environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system's display layout and font rendering shall scale correctly to the resolution and aspect ratio of the connected projection equipment, maintaining the fixed 2:1 layout proportion and DHS readability standards across different display configurations.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 and OC-03 require correct rendering on projection equipment; resolution-independent scaling ensures readability across varied projection setups.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all user authentication events (successful logins, failed login attempts, and logouts) with timestamps and user identifiers, and these logs shall be accessible to the IT Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: DHS security policy and Business Objective 7 require a verifiable record of all access attempts to support security auditing and incident investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent use by multiple authorized users on separate client PCs within the same exercise operations center, with each instance independently displaying current exercise data without data conflicts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple stakeholder roles (controllers, directors, planners) may run simultaneous instances; the system must support this without degradation or conflicts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an installation and configuration guide sufficient for an IT Administrator to deploy, configure, and verify the system on a new client PC within one business day.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational constraint OC-01 and the IT Administrator stakeholder role require that the system be deployable quickly to support exercise readiness timelines.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate the structural integrity and completeness of MSEL Summary data received from each source system during every refresh cycle and shall log a warning in the audit trail if data is malformed, missing required fields, or inconsistent with the expected data contract.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-02 requires consistently structured MSEL data; runtime validation protects display accuracy and provides an audit record of data quality issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an administrator interface allowing the IT Administrator to manage the list of authorized users (add, deactivate, and assign roles), integrating with or consuming input from the externally managed identity directory without requiring EEMDS to serve as the identity management system of record.
generated_requirements.rationale: Assumption A-04 and the IT Administrator stakeholder role require a user management interface that delegates identity management to an external directory while enforcing access within EEMDS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require every user to authenticate with valid credentials (username and password) before accessing any system function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and the security success criterion mandate that 100% of users are authenticated and authorized before any access is granted.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deny access to any individual who is not formally registered in the organization's realtor registry, returning a clear rejection message.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 requires that unregistered individuals are denied access at all entry points to protect proprietary MLS and client data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authorize each authenticated user against the current realtor registry on every session initiation to confirm continued registration status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous authorization enforcement ensures that deregistered realtors cannot access the system using previously valid credentials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to search MLS property listings by specifying one or more criteria (e.g., location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms).
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS query capability is a core in-scope feature required to eliminate the field productivity gap described in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS search results including at least one clear property image per listing, address, price, and key property attributes.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD scope specifies that search results must include clear property images to support on-site client consultations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS property search results within 30 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions, 100% of the time.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 30-second search result ceiling is a hard performance constraint defined in both the business objectives and technical constraints sections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save property search results to local device storage for later review, including in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 requires that saved search results remain accessible when the device is offline, supporting uninterrupted field productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to create new property listings in the MLS from the mobile device, submitting all required listing fields.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS listing management (create and update) is explicitly listed as an in-scope capability in Section 6.1.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to update existing MLS property listings from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS listing update capability is an in-scope feature required to keep listing data current from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all property listing data against defined MLS data validation rules before submitting a create or update operation to the MLS, rejecting malformed or incomplete entries with descriptive error messages.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 requires data validation controls to prevent incorrect or malformed data from being introduced into MLS records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) report based on property criteria explicitly specified by the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 mandates that CMA reports are only generated upon explicit criteria input from the user, and CMA generation is a core in-scope feature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not generate a CMA report unless the realtor has explicitly entered property criteria; the generate action must be blocked if required criteria fields are empty.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 explicitly prohibits generating a CMA report without explicit criteria input to ensure report accuracy and relevance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a completed CMA report to the realtor within 60 seconds of criteria entry submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 60-second CMA generation ceiling is a hard performance constraint defined in the business objectives and technical constraints sections.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to save generated CMA reports to local device storage, making them accessible in offline mode.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 specifies that previously downloaded CMA reports must be accessible when internet connectivity is unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a map showing property locations for a specified geographic area, using data from the integrated third-party mapping provider.
generated_requirements.rationale: Property mapping is an in-scope feature (Section 6.1) and BR-09 ties map availability to MLS-accessible properties for the authenticated realtor.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide turn-by-turn driving directions to a selected property from within the application, without requiring the realtor to use a separate mapping tool.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated driving directions are an in-scope feature designed to eliminate the navigation overhead identified as a pain point in the problem statement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict map and driving direction functionality to properties that exist within the MLS dataset accessible to the authenticated realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 explicitly limits map and directions to MLS-accessible properties to enforce data access boundaries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the realtor's client contacts from the office system, allowing the realtor to view contact details from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Contact management is an in-scope feature required to eliminate the data fragmentation pain point where agents work from memory or paper notes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall download and display the realtor's appointments from the office system, allowing the realtor to view scheduled appointments from the mobile device.
generated_requirements.rationale: Appointment management is an in-scope feature required to keep agents informed of their schedule while in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically synchronize contact and appointment data with the office system once every hour without requiring any action from the realtor.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates automatic hourly synchronization to ensure contacts and appointments are never more than 60 minutes stale.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall notify the realtor of any new appointments discovered during each automatic synchronization cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires that realtors are notified of new appointments found during synchronization to improve appointment responsiveness.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall initiate calls to sellers or buyers to schedule appointments on behalf of the realtor, using contact phone numbers from the realtor's client records.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated appointment scheduling calls are an in-scope feature (Section 6.1) and BR-10 governs their initiation and traceability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict appointment-related calls to contacts that are associated with the authenticated realtor's own client records only.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires that communications are traceable to the realtor's account and limited to their own client contacts to prevent unauthorized contact.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request initiated by the system, without requiring the realtor to manually check for updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 and the appointment responsiveness success criterion require automated push notification upon client response to eliminate scheduling lag.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate in an offline read-only mode when internet connectivity is unavailable, allowing realtors to access previously downloaded search results, CMA reports, contacts, and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 and the future state description require offline read access to cached data to ensure uninterrupted field productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue write operations (e.g., new listings, listing updates) that cannot be completed while offline and automatically submit them to the appropriate backend system upon restoration of internet connectivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 requires that write operations unable to complete offline are queued for submission upon reconnection to prevent data loss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect the transition from offline to online connectivity and automatically trigger submission of all queued write operations without requiring realtor intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic reconnection handling is essential to the offline-to-online transition behaviour identified as a high-risk gap in the elicitation strategy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall save and persist all user preferences (including search filters, display settings, and saved results) across sessions and system failures, restoring 100% of saved preferences upon system recovery.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and the data integrity success criterion require complete restoration of user preferences after any failure or connectivity interruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours per day, 365 days per year, achieving a minimum availability rate of 99.9% (no more than 1 failure per 1,000 access attempts).
generated_requirements.rationale: The 99.9% availability success criterion and technical constraint require continuous system uptime to support agents operating at all hours.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,000 simultaneous authenticated users at launch without degradation of performance SLAs.
generated_requirements.rationale: The scalability success criterion requires the system to handle the current agent population of 1,000 concurrent users from day one.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to scale to support 5,000 simultaneous users within 12 months of launch without requiring a system redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The fivefold growth projection and scalability success criterion require an architecture that can expand capacity without a redesign.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall run natively on devices operating Windows CE and Palm operating systems; no other mobile platforms are in scope for this release.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraints section explicitly restricts the target platforms to Windows CE and Palm OS for this release.

generated_requirements.text: The application user interface shall conform to the native UI standards and conventions of the target smart device platform, including standard menus and navigation buttons.
generated_requirements.rationale: The technical constraints require platform-native UI conformance to ensure intuitive operation consistent with the device's standard interaction model.

generated_requirements.text: At least 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to successfully install the application on their smart device without printed instructions or IT assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: The self-service installation success criterion and operational constraint require that the installation process is self-explanatory for untrained users.

generated_requirements.text: The application shall be usable by realtors who have received no formal training on the product; all UI terminology must be natural to the real estate profession.
generated_requirements.rationale: The no-training-requirement operational constraint and stakeholder needs require a self-explanatory interface using domain-familiar language.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce authentication and authorization on every access attempt, with zero unauthorized access incidents permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: The security success criterion requires 100% authentication and authorization coverage with zero unauthorized access incidents.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the mobile application and backend systems (office sync server, MLS API, authentication server) shall be encrypted in transit using industry-standard protocols.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting proprietary MLS and client data during transmission is essential given the security constraints and the sensitivity of the data accessed.

generated_requirements.text: User credentials and authentication tokens stored on the device shall be encrypted at rest using platform-supported secure storage mechanisms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Securing credentials on the device prevents unauthorized access if the device is lost or stolen, supporting the zero-unauthorized-access objective.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with the MLS via its programmatic API to perform property listing queries, retrieve listing images, and submit new or updated listings.
generated_requirements.rationale: The entire MLS search and listing management capability depends on the MLS API integration identified as dependency D-02.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with the office synchronization system via its documented API or data feed to perform hourly two-way synchronization of contacts and appointments.
generated_requirements.rationale: The office system integration is dependency D-01 and is the foundation of the automatic synchronization capability required by BR-03.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with a licensed third-party mapping data provider to render property location maps and generate driving directions within the application.
generated_requirements.rationale: The mapping and directions feature depends on a third-party mapping service (dependency D-03 and assumption A-06) as the data source.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall use the smart device's native telephony capability to initiate calls to sellers or buyers for appointment scheduling purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create a Program of Study by specifying a unique program name and a required set of classes, each classified as either clinical or non-clinical.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 mandates that every cohort is bound to exactly one Program of Study with a defined class set.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to modify an existing Program of Study, including updating its name and adding or removing required classes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Programs of Study must remain maintainable over the system's minimum five-year operational lifespan.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create a cohort record uniquely identified by its start month and year, and associate it with exactly one Program of Study.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 requires cohorts to be uniquely identified by start month/year and bound to one Program of Study.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to define and modify a preferred class sequence for a cohort, specifying the quarter in which each required class will be offered.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 requires a definable preferred sequence including the offering quarter for each class in a cohort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce that only students who are eligible for a cohort's Program of Study may be added to that cohort, and shall display an error message if an ineligible student is selected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.1 explicitly prohibits adding ineligible students to a cohort.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify every class record as either clinical or non-clinical, and shall prevent a class from being saved without exactly one classification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 states no class may be both or neither classification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that a non-clinical class record specifies, at minimum, the course name, lecture room requirements, and instructor needs before the record can be saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 defines the minimum required attributes for a non-clinical class.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall require that a clinical class record specifies, at minimum, the course name, lecture room requirements, clinical site needs, lecture instructor needs, and clinical lab instructor needs before the record can be saved.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 defines the minimum required attributes for a clinical class.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect scheduling conflicts — defined as two classes assigned to the same cohort with overlapping days and times — and shall prevent any conflicting schedule from being saved, displaying a descriptive conflict message to the user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.2 and Objective 1 require automated conflict detection and prevention as a core scheduling safeguard.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members to create a clinical lab section only for a clinical class that already exists in the system, specifying the clinical site name, assigned instructor, and day and time of the lab.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 restricts clinical lab section creation to existing clinical classes and mandates minimum required fields.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent cancellation of a clinical lab section if any students are currently enrolled in that section, and shall display the count of enrolled students to the user attempting the cancellation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 explicitly prohibits cancellation of a lab section with enrolled students.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a student from being added to a clinical lab section unless that student is already registered for the corresponding clinical class.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 requires class registration as a prerequisite for clinical lab section enrollment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a student to be moved from one clinical lab section to another, provided both sections are associated with the same clinical class, and shall update all relevant enrollment records upon confirmation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.3 explicitly permits inter-section student moves within the same clinical class.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store a nursing student record containing at minimum: personal information fields, student status (full-time or part-time), program enrollment, and cohort assignment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Business Rule 5.4 require comprehensive student records including status and cohort data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Nursing Staff Members and Program Administrators to modify a part-time student's class sequence and timeline independently of the standard cohort sequence, with changes immediately reflected in cohort summaries.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.4 and the future-state description require per-student sequence deviation management for part-time students.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when a student's status changes (e.g., from full-time to part-time), using stored email addresses as the delivery channel.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rules 5.4 and 5.6 mandate automated notification upon student status change.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when a student's upcoming class schedule is published or updated.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 lists schedule publication and update as mandatory notification trigger events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when a clinical lab section's capacity changes or the section is cancelled.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 lists clinical section capacity change and cancellation as mandatory notification trigger events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when a class is cancelled, its section details change, or its offering for a given quarter changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 lists class cancellation and section/offering changes as mandatory notification trigger events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when a cohort's Program of Study sequence changes or a specific week's schedule is modified (e.g., lab cancelled due to instructor illness).
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 lists cohort sequence and weekly schedule changes as mandatory notification trigger events.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to all affected parties when a student is moved to a different clinical lab section.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.6 lists student clinical section reassignment as a mandatory notification trigger event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a contact information repository storing email addresses and phone numbers for all relevant parties: Nursing Staff Members, students, lecture instructors, clinical lab instructors, and clinical site administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Business Rule 5.6 require stored contact information as the basis for all outbound communications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a centralized clinical site repository containing each site's name, contact person, and contact information, accessible only to authorized Nursing Department staff.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and Business Rule 5.5 require a centralized, access-controlled clinical site record.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members to view all clinical lab sections scheduled at a given clinical site for any specified quarter.
generated_requirements.rationale: The future-state description requires administrators to view all labs at a site per quarter from a centralized repository.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate on-demand cross-cohort quarterly planning reports listing all classes needed for a specified quarter, including sections, labs, and room types, without requiring manual data aggregation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 5 and Section 6.1 require on-demand cross-cohort planning reports to replace manual aggregation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate printable summary reports for individual cohorts, clinical sites, clinical lab sections, individual students, and the system log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 explicitly lists printable summaries for these entities as in-scope reporting capabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a system log recording all significant user actions and system events, and shall provide a printable summary of the log to authorized administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 includes system log maintenance and printable log summary as in-scope capabilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with CampusConnect's central server to support the final class section scheduling function, with this interface accessible only to Dr. Susan Poslusny and Karen Sysol.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 and Section 6.1 require a CampusConnect integration restricted to named authorized users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with the faculty central server to synchronize faculty data, reducing manual re-entry of instructor information.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and the future-state description require integration with the faculty central server for data consistency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall interface with the student master server to retrieve and synchronize student eligibility and enrollment data, reducing manual re-entry and improving record accuracy.
generated_requirements.rationale: Section 6.1 and the future-state description require integration with the student master server for data consistency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access controls such that clinical site details (including contact person and contact information) are accessible only to Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 restricts clinical site detail access to Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members only.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access controls such that student personal information is accessible only to Dr. Susan Poslusny and Dr. Julie Donalek, and shall deny access to all other users including other Program Administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 names specific individuals authorized for student personal information access, excluding all others.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access controls such that the CampusConnect interface function is accessible only to Dr. Susan Poslusny and Karen Sysol.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.5 restricts the CampusConnect interface to two named individuals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall perform basic data integrity validation on all data entry fields, rejecting and flagging invalid or incomplete data before it is persisted to the database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Business Rule 5.7 mandates basic data integrity validation on all data entry to prevent incorrect data storage.

generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 2 KPI requires the system to handle all nursing students and classes for a ten-year horizon.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce technical access controls that produce zero unauthorized access incidents to protected data categories: student personal information, clinical site details, and the CampusConnect interface.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via a logon mechanism before granting access to any application function; only users with valid, active accounts shall be permitted to log on.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates that only users with valid, active accounts may access the system, establishing authentication as a gate to all functionality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control with at minimum two access levels — View and Update — such that only users assigned Update access may initiate, create, or modify dispute cases, while View-only users may search and view cases exclusively.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 and BR-06 require that dispute initiation and modification are restricted to Update-access users, with View-only users limited to read operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict each user's access to dispute cases belonging exclusively to their authorized cardholder base; any attempt to access a dispute case outside the user's authorized portfolio shall be blocked and logged.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 prohibits cross-portfolio access, which is a critical control for both security and regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prohibit external users from requesting original receipts; the 'Request for Original Receipt' action shall be available only to internal users.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 explicitly restricts original receipt requests to internal users, requiring the system to enforce this boundary programmatically.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow revoked user accounts to be reinstated only by a user with the System Administrator role; no other role shall have the capability to reinstate a revoked account.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 designates the System Administrator as the sole authority for account reinstatement to prevent unauthorized re-activation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall dynamically filter and present the list of available follow-up actions for a dispute case based on (a) the current status of the case and (b) the access level of the logged-in user; actions not applicable to the case status or user role shall not be displayed or selectable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-06 requires that follow-up action menus are context-sensitive to both case state and user authorization level.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present only transactions that fall within the eligible dispute age window — as defined by Regulation E, Regulation Z, and the Merchant Operating Regulations — when a user initiates a dispute; ineligible transactions shall be filtered out and not selectable.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 mandates that only disputable transactions within the regulatory age window are presented to prevent non-compliant dispute initiations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall permit a single dispute case to be initiated against multiple transactions, provided all selected transactions belong to a single merchant.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 allows multi-transaction disputes under the constraint that all transactions share the same merchant, reflecting operational workflow needs.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect duplicate dispute requests by checking whether an active or closed dispute already exists for a given transaction; if a duplicate is detected, the system shall block the new dispute initiation and display an informative message to the user identifying the existing dispute case number.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 requires 100% prevention of duplicate disputes to avoid erroneous financial adjustments and reconciliation problems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support three types of ticket retrieval requests: (1) Request for Original Receipt, (2) Request for a Copy of the Receipt, and (3) Request for a Portfolio; each type shall enforce its own eligibility rules, including the restriction that Original Receipt requests are available to internal users only.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 defines three distinct retrieval request types with separate eligibility rules that must be enforced programmatically.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support two types of chargeback requests — Pending and Immediate — and shall prompt the user to provide all required information specific to the selected chargeback type before submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 requires the system to guide users through type-specific data collection for chargeback requests to ensure completeness.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful creation of a ticket retrieval request, the system shall display a confirmation screen containing: the assigned dispute case number, the type of retrieval requested, and the date by which the merchant response is due.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 specifies the mandatory confirmation data elements that must be presented to the user after a retrieval request is created.

generated_requirements.text: Upon successful creation of a chargeback request, the system shall display a confirmation screen containing: the assigned dispute case number, the chargeback type (Pending or Immediate), and the date by which the merchant response is due.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 specifies the mandatory confirmation data elements that must be presented to the user after a chargeback request is created.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall dynamically derive and present only eligible dispute reason codes based on (a) the type of transaction being disputed and (b) the industry classification of the merchant involved; free-form or unconstrained reason code entry shall not be permitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 mandates context-sensitive reason code filtering to ensure regulatory compliance and prevent invalid dispute classifications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically record a case history entry for every action — whether user-initiated or system-initiated — that creates or modifies a dispute case; each entry shall capture the user's name or system identifier, the date and time of the action, and a description of the action taken.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-15 requires a comprehensive, tamper-evident audit trail on every dispute case to support internal auditing and regulatory examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record all additions of new user accounts and all modifications to existing users' access levels in a User Report that is accessible for audit review.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 requires a dedicated User Report to capture account lifecycle events for compliance and audit traceability.

generated_requirements.text: When an automated (non-user-initiated) system process acts on a dispute case, the system shall queue a notification for the user who originally initiated that dispute case; the notification shall be displayed to that user upon their next logon.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-17 ensures that originating representatives are made aware of automated case changes so they can take timely follow-up action.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically purge dispute cases that have been in a closed status for more than 6 months from the online disputes database; this purge process shall run on a scheduled, automated basis without requiring manual intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 mandates automated data retention management to maintain system performance and control storage costs.

generated_requirements.text: All business rules implemented in the Disputes System — including dispute eligibility windows, chargeback procedures, and notification timelines — shall comply with the requirements of Regulation E (Electronic Fund Transfer Act) and Regulation Z (Truth in Lending Act) as interpreted and approved by the organization's legal and compliance team.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 and C-01 require programmatic enforcement of federal regulatory rules to eliminate compliance exposure.

generated_requirements.text: All business rules implemented in the Disputes System — including chargeback procedures and retrieval request handling — shall comply with the Merchant Operating Regulations as interpreted and approved by the organization's legal and compliance team.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-20 and C-02 require that merchant-facing dispute procedures conform to the Merchant Operating Regulations.

generated_requirements.text: All letter requests generated within the Disputes System shall be formatted in accordance with the interface specifications of the Print Letter Utility and submitted exclusively to that utility; the Disputes application shall not generate or print letters independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-21 and C-07 establish the Print Letter Utility as the sole letter generation system and define the formatting contract.

generated_requirements.text: All financial adjustment requests to cardmember or merchant accounts shall be submitted exclusively through the Cardmember Posting and Billing System interface; the Disputes application shall not post adjustments directly to any account.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 and C-08 designate the Cardmember Posting and Billing System as the sole channel for financial adjustments to maintain accounting integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dispute case search function that supports searching by: dispute case number, merchant account number, cardmember account number, and issuer number; and filtering by: date range, dispute type, case status, and reason code.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD scope section defines these search and filter dimensions as required capabilities for representatives to locate cases efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display dispute case search results with color-coded status indicators, including at minimum: yellow for open cases and gray for closed cases, enabling representatives to instantly assess case status across the portfolio.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD future state and scope specify color-coded results to provide real-time visual case status visibility without additional navigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retrieve cardmember account information exclusively from the Cardmember Information Database in real time; the Disputes application shall not maintain a local copy of cardmember master data.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-04 prohibits the Disputes application from maintaining its own cardmember data to ensure a single authoritative source.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retrieve transaction and statement data exclusively from the Statement Database in real time; the Disputes application shall not maintain its own transaction records.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-05 prohibits local transaction data storage to prevent data divergence and ensure accuracy during dispute initiation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retrieve merchant information exclusively from the Merchant Information Database in real time; the Disputes application shall not maintain its own merchant master data.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-06 prohibits local merchant data storage to maintain a single authoritative source for merchant classification and details.

generated_requirements.text: The System Administrator shall be able to add new user accounts, assign or modify access levels (View or Update), revoke user accounts, and reinstate revoked accounts through the Disputes application's administration interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD scope and stakeholder sections define user lifecycle management as a core System Administrator capability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent end users from accessing any underlying data files or databases directly; all data interaction shall occur exclusively through the Disputes application's user interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-09 mandates that all data access is mediated through the application to enforce access controls and maintain audit integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the complete case history — including all user-initiated and system-initiated actions with actor identity, date/time, and action description — to authorized users viewing a dispute case.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD audit trail objective requires that the full case history is visible to authorized users to support case management and audit review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully handle unavailability of any integrated external system (Statement Database, Cardmember Information Database, Merchant Information Database, Print Letter Utility, Cardmember Posting and Billing System) by displaying a clear, user-friendly error message and preventing partial or inconsistent data submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 through D-03 identify integration dependencies that can impact functionality; failure handling must be defined to protect data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall conform to the organization's corporate standards for user interface creation for all screens accessible by both internal and external users; no non-standard UI frameworks shall be used without explicit approval.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-03 mandates adherence to corporate UI standards to ensure consistency, accessibility, and compliance with organizational design guidelines.

generated_requirements.text: Screen-to-screen navigation response time shall not exceed 5 seconds under any load condition within the supported concurrency range of up to 350 concurrent users, measured end-to-end from user action to full screen render.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-11 and Objective 5 set a hard 5-second response time ceiling to ensure representative productivity is not impaired under peak load.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 350 concurrent users performing dispute initiation, search, and follow-up actions simultaneously without measurable degradation in response time or data integrity.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-10 and Objective 5 define 350 concurrent users as the minimum scalability threshold for the production environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year, with the only permitted planned downtime being: Monday through Saturday, 3:00 AM to 4:00 AM EST, and Sunday, 1:00 AM to 5:00 AM EST.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-12 and Objective 5 mandate near-continuous availability to support a 24-hour multi-user operational environment.

generated_requirements.text: The system's unplanned (unscheduled) downtime shall not exceed 0.1% of total available hours in any rolling 12-month period, excluding the defined scheduled maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: The 24×7×365 availability objective implies a high reliability target; an explicit unplanned downtime ceiling makes this measurable and testable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed such that 100% of cardmember and merchant services representatives can successfully perform their core job functions — dispute initiation and follow-up actions respectively — on their first attempt following a standard 2-day training course.
generated_requirements.rationale: C-14 and Objective 6 set a learnability standard that constrains UI complexity and workflow design to enable rapid, error-free adoption.

generated_requirements.text: All user passwords and authentication credentials shall be stored using a one-way cryptographic hashing algorithm with a unique salt per credential; plaintext passwords shall never be stored or transmitted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Secure credential storage is a baseline security requirement implied by the authentication mandate in BR-02 and the organization's compliance obligations.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the Disputes application and external users (partner institution representatives) shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-08 notes that external users access the system over a secure network; the application must enforce transport-layer encryption to protect sensitive dispute and cardmember data.

generated_requirements.text: All unauthorized access attempts — including failed authentication, attempts to access out-of-portfolio cases, and attempts to invoke restricted actions — shall be logged with the user identifier, timestamp, and nature of the attempt, and shall be available for security review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 3 requires 100% blocking of unauthorized access attempts; logging these events is essential for security monitoring and audit evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The case history audit trail shall be immutable; once a case history entry is written, it shall not be modifiable or deletable by any user, including System Administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Objective 4 requires a tamper-evident audit trail; immutability is the technical mechanism that ensures legal and regulatory defensibility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce session timeout by automatically terminating an inactive user session after a configurable inactivity period (default: 15 minutes), requiring re-authentication before access is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout is a standard security control to prevent unauthorized access via unattended terminals in a multi-user representative environment.

generated_requirements.text: The automated data purge process for dispute cases closed more than 6 months shall complete its scheduled execution within a defined maintenance window without impacting online system availability or response time for active users.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-18 mandates automated purging; the purge must be designed to avoid performance impact on concurrent users during its execution.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with a modular, layered architecture that allows individual business rule modules (e.g., reason code derivation, eligibility filtering) to be updated independently to accommodate future regulatory changes without requiring full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Regulations E, Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations are subject to change; a maintainable architecture reduces the cost and risk of future compliance updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a User Report that can be generated on demand by the System Administrator and internal auditors, listing all user account additions and access level modifications with actor identity, date/time, and change detail, covering the full history of the system.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 and Objective 4 require a User Report as an auditable record of all account lifecycle events for compliance examination.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data entered during dispute initiation and follow-up actions in real time, providing immediate inline error messages that identify the specific field and the nature of the validation failure before allowing submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time validation supports the 2-day training adoption goal by guiding representatives to correct errors immediately, reducing re-work and handle time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and operable on the organization's existing network and hosting infrastructure without requiring modifications to that infrastructure, provided the infrastructure meets the stated capacity requirements (350 concurrent users, sub-5-second response, 24×7 availability).
generated_requirements.rationale: A-07 assumes the existing infrastructure is capable; the system must be designed to operate within that environment without imposing infrastructure changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide real-time integration with the Statement Database, Cardmember Information Database, and Merchant Information Database such that data retrieved during dispute initiation reflects the current state of those systems at the time of the request, with a maximum data staleness of zero (no caching of master data).
generated_requirements.rationale: C-04 through C-06 and the future state description require real-time data retrieval from authoritative source systems to eliminate re-keying errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall assign a unique, system-generated dispute case number to every successfully created dispute case; this number shall be immutable after assignment and shall serve as the primary identifier for all subsequent case actions, searches, and audit entries.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 and BR-13 reference the dispute case number in confirmations; a unique, immutable identifier is required for traceability and duplicate detection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all integration calls to external systems (Statement Database, Cardmember Information Database, Merchant Information Database, Print Letter Utility, Cardmember Posting and Billing System), including request timestamp, response status, and response time, to support operational monitoring and incident diagnosis.
generated_requirements.rationale: D-01 through D-03 identify integration dependencies as operational risks; integration logging enables rapid diagnosis of failures and supports SLA monitoring.
