ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide a confirmation to the user upon the creation of a chargeback request. The confirmation must contain the following information; the dispute case number the type of chargeback requested (pending or immediate) and the date that the merchant response is due.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall prevent the creation of duplicate dispute requests 100% of the time.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall accurately provide a list of possible reason codes for a given transaction. The list of possible reason codes must be derived based on the type of transaction and the industry that the merchant belongs to according to the business rules defined for the Disputes System.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide the ability to view existing dispute cases.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System must provide Search functionality. The search method must include the ability to search by (1) the dispute case number (2) the merchant account number (3) the cardmember account number and (4) the issuer number. In addition to the above criteria the search functionality must further allow the user to limit the results of the search by a date range the type of dispute (ticket retrieval request or chargeback notification) the case status (open closed or all) and the dispute reason code.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: A list of all disputes cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color -coding the line item in the list. For example all open cases will be yellow and all closed cases will be gray.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall provide the users the ability to view the history of all activities that have occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Dispute System shall provide the users with the ability to follow up on a dispute case via follow-up actions. The list of available follow up actions for a given dispute case must be filtered by the status of the case and the access level of the user.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: The Disputes System shall record the name of the user and the date for any activity that creates or modifies the disputes case in the system. A detailed history of the actions taken on the case including the date and the user that performed the action must be maintained for auditing purposes.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: For any systematic (non-user initiated) action that occurs on a case such as the disputes aging process a case activity will be generated. Upon the next logon the user that initiated the dispute case must be notified that a systematic action has occurred on the dispute case.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

ground_truth_requirements.text: Any disputes cases that have been closed for over 6 months must be purged from the online disputes database.
ground_truth_requirements.label: FR

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to the recycled parts search function exclusively to users assigned the Collision Estimator role; all other roles shall receive an access-denied response if they attempt to invoke this function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 mandates that only Collision Estimators may search for recycled parts, enforcing role-based workflow separation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a Collision Estimator to search for available recycled parts by entering vehicle information (year, make, model) and damaged parts information, and shall return matching results sourced from the nightly ChoiceParts data feed.
generated_requirements.rationale: This is the core estimator workflow described in the future-state capabilities and BR-05.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall first execute the recycled parts search against the Collision Estimator's configured list of preferred parts suppliers; if no matching parts are found, the system shall automatically and transparently expand the search to all suppliers within a 30-mile radius of the vehicle's ZIP code without requiring additional user action.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-05 and BR-06 define the two-stage preferred-supplier-then-radius fallback search logic.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a Collision Estimator from applying recycled parts to a collision estimate when the recycled parts search returns zero available parts, and shall display an informative message explaining why the action is blocked.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-07 prohibits applying parts when none are available, and clear messaging supports usability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall persist the complete set of recycled parts search results associated with each estimate at the time the search is performed, and shall make these stored results retrievable at any future point for use in audit report generation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-08 requires retention of original search results to support accurate audit comparison.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to the recycled parts audit report request function exclusively to users assigned the Adjuster or Supervisor Adjuster role; Collision Estimators and all other roles shall not have access to this function.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-02 mandates that only Adjusters may request audit reports, enforcing role-based access control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate a recycled parts audit report for a specified estimate that includes: (a) the total number of recycled parts used in the estimate, (b) the total number of available recycled parts from the original search, (c) the percentage of available recycled parts that were actually used, and (d) a total audit score calculated as the sum of individual line-item scores comparing each available part against its usage status on the estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-14 and BR-15 define the audit report content and scoring structure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall make generated audit reports accessible to any Adjuster or Supervisor Adjuster, not only the user who originally requested the report, and shall persist reports so they remain retrievable at later points in time.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-16 requires shared, persistent audit report access across the adjuster community.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an Adjuster to search for a preferred repair facility by providing vehicle information (year, make, model), vehicle location (street address, city, state, ZIP code), and a search radius between 1 and 30 miles.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-09 defines the required input parameters for the preferred repair facility search.

generated_requirements.text: When a ZIP code is not provided by the user, the system shall automatically resolve the ZIP code from the supplied street address, city, and state using an integrated address resolution service before executing the repair facility search.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-10 requires ZIP code resolution from partial address data to prevent search failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return the repair facility with the highest aggregate rating that matches the vehicle location and radius search criteria as the recommended preferred repair facility for the estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-11 defines the selection algorithm for the preferred repair facility recommendation.

generated_requirements.text: When no rated repair facility exists within the specified search radius, the system shall return a list of all repair facilities within that radius and prompt the Adjuster to manually select one, rather than returning an error or empty result.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-12 defines the graceful fallback behavior when no rated facility is available.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow an Adjuster to override the system-recommended preferred repair facility on an estimate and assign a different facility from the available results, recording the override action and the identity of the overriding user in the estimate's audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-13 permits adjuster override, and audit trail capture supports SOX and compliance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict the ability to create or modify preferred repair facility ratings exclusively to users assigned the Supervisor Adjuster role; Adjusters and Collision Estimators shall be able to view ratings but shall receive an access-denied response if they attempt to create or modify them.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-03 mandates that only Supervisor Adjusters may update ratings, protecting data integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow a Supervisor Adjuster to enter and save ratings for a repair facility on a scale of 1 to 10 across rating categories that include, at minimum, 'attempted use of recycled parts' and 'actual use of recycled parts', and shall support additional configurable rating categories.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-17 and BR-18 define the rating scale and minimum required categories.

generated_requirements.text: When a Supervisor Adjuster accesses the ratings form for a repair facility that has no previously recorded ratings, the system shall display a blank, editable ratings form rather than an error message or empty-state error.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-19 requires graceful handling of the new-facility ratings scenario to prevent user confusion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize recycled parts data from the ChoiceParts external data feed once per day at approximately 1:00 AM, replacing or updating the local data store with the latest available parts inventory.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-22 and TC-11 define the nightly ChoiceParts synchronization schedule.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all user-entered data against defined field-level rules (type, range, format, and mandatory constraints) before persisting any record, and shall display specific, actionable validation error messages to the user for each failed validation.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-21 requires data validation to prevent invalid data from being stored.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce company-level data isolation such that a user authenticated under one insurance company's tenant can never view, search, or retrieve claim data, estimate data, audit reports, or repair facility ratings belonging to another insurance company's tenant, regardless of shared infrastructure.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 mandates strict multi-tenant data isolation to prevent legal and regulatory exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit trail that records, at minimum, the user identity, timestamp, action type, and affected data entity for all create, update, delete, and access operations on estimates, audit reports, repair facility assignments, and ratings.
generated_requirements.rationale: SOX compliance (TC-12) and estimatics law compliance (TC-13) require a complete, auditable data lineage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide in-system contextual help content on at least 90% of application screens, accessible without navigating away from the current screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table and scope section require 90% help screen coverage to support learnability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and handle ChoiceParts data feed failures gracefully by: (a) retaining the most recently successfully synchronized data set for use during business hours, (b) logging the failure with full diagnostic detail, and (c) alerting the operations team so that manual intervention can be initiated.
generated_requirements.rationale: A-05 acknowledges feed failures are possible; graceful degradation prevents total estimator workflow blockage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a clearly visible data-staleness indicator to Collision Estimators when the recycled parts data being searched is more than 24 hours old due to a missed or failed ChoiceParts synchronization.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-11 acknowledges data can be up to 24 hours old; users must be informed to make compliant decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be delivered as a web-based application fully functional in Internet Explorer 5.5 and above and Mozilla Firefox 1.0 and above, with no browser plug-ins or client-side installations required beyond those already present in a standard corporate browser deployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-02 and the scope section mandate support for these specific browser versions.

generated_requirements.text: The recycled parts search function shall return results to the user within 15 seconds under normal operating load, measured from the moment the user submits the search request to the moment results are rendered in the browser.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table specifies a 15-second response time target for recycled parts search.

generated_requirements.text: The preferred repair facility search function shall return results to the user within 8 seconds under normal operating load, measured from search submission to results rendering.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table specifies an 8-second response time target for preferred repair facility search.

generated_requirements.text: The audit report generation function shall return the completed report to the requesting user within 10 seconds under normal operating load, measured from report request submission to full report display.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table specifies a 10-second response time target for audit report generation.

generated_requirements.text: The repair facility ratings save operation shall complete and confirm success to the user within 5 seconds under normal operating load.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table specifies a 5-second save completion target for ratings.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 1,500 simultaneous authenticated users at launch without degradation of the defined response time SLAs for any function.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-07 and the KPI table require 1,500 concurrent user capacity at launch.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall be designed to scale to support 10,000 simultaneous authenticated users within two years of launch without requiring a fundamental redesign of the application or data tier.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-07 and the KPI table require a scalability path to 10,000 concurrent users by Year 2.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be capable of processing and storing approximately 50,000 recycled parts records per day during the nightly ChoiceParts synchronization without exceeding the defined maintenance window or impacting daytime system availability.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table specifies a daily data throughput requirement of 50,000 recycled parts records.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve 98% uptime during all scheduled availability windows, measured monthly, with unplanned downtime not exceeding 2% of scheduled available time.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-08 and the BRD KPI table define a 98% uptime SLA.

generated_requirements.text: All system maintenance activities, including deployments, upgrades, database maintenance, and infrastructure patching, shall be scheduled and executed exclusively within the established corporate maintenance windows and shall not impact system availability outside those windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-09 requires all maintenance to occur within defined corporate windows.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) by authenticating every user session against the corporate identity and access management system and authorizing each function invocation against the user's assigned role before permitting access.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-01 through BR-03 and TC-12 require RBAC enforcement integrated with corporate IAM.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement multi-tenant data isolation at the data access layer such that all database queries, API calls, and report generation operations are automatically scoped to the authenticated user's insurance company tenant, with no possibility of cross-tenant data leakage through any application pathway.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-04 mandates absolute company-level data isolation in the multi-carrier deployment model.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be free of known computer viruses and malware at the time of each deployment, and shall implement technical controls to protect against denial-of-service attacks, including rate limiting, connection throttling, and infrastructure-level DDoS mitigation.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-15 explicitly requires virus-free delivery and protection against malicious attacks including DoS.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the user's browser and the system, and between the system and the ChoiceParts integration endpoint, shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit is required to protect sensitive claims and parts data and support SOX and insurance regulation compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to the corporate User Interface Guidelines and corporate color scheme standards as documented and provided by the Corporate Architecture & UI Standards Team.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-04 mandates adherence to corporate UI guidelines and color scheme standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface for Collision Estimator-facing functions shall be operable in physically demanding repair facility environments (dirty, noisy, limited workspace) by presenting large touch-targets, high-contrast visuals, and minimal required data entry steps.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-06 constrains the interface design for estimator functions to be usable in harsh repair shop conditions.

generated_requirements.text: A new user shall be able to achieve full proficiency in all functions relevant to their assigned role within two days of completing the provided onsite training program, as measured by a training completion assessment.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table specifies a two-day learnability target as a key success criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall adhere to the corporate Architecture guidelines governing technology stack selection, integration patterns, and deployment architecture, as documented and provided by the Corporate Architecture Team prior to development start.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-03 mandates compliance with corporate architecture guidelines to ensure long-term maintainability and supportability.

generated_requirements.text: All product installations and upgrades shall be executable exclusively by the middleware technology team using documented deployment procedures, within established corporate launch timeframes, without requiring involvement from the application development team.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-05 restricts deployment authority to the middleware team, requiring deployable, documented release packages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be supportable through the existing corporate support center without requiring a dedicated support team, meaning all diagnostic information, error logs, and user account data shall be accessible through standard corporate support tooling.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-10 and A-02 require the system to be supportable within existing corporate support infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall operate entirely on existing corporate hardware infrastructure across development, test, and production environments, with no requirement for new hardware procurement.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-01 constrains the system to run on existing hardware with no new procurement budget.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act requirements applicable to financial controls and auditability of claims-related data, including retention of audit trail records for the period required by SOX and the ability to produce audit trail reports for regulatory examination.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-12 mandates SOX compliance, particularly for financial controls and claims data auditability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable estimatics laws governing the use of recycled parts in collision repair estimates in each jurisdiction where it is deployed, and shall provide audit trail data sufficient to demonstrate compliance during regulatory examination.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-13 mandates compliance with estimatics laws and requires an audit trail supporting regulatory review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with all applicable insurance regulations governing claims processing in each jurisdiction where it is deployed, and shall be reviewed against updated regulatory guidance prior to each major release.
generated_requirements.rationale: TC-14 mandates compliance with insurance regulations across all deployment jurisdictions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all ChoiceParts data feed synchronization events, including start time, end time, record counts processed, records rejected, and any error conditions, and shall make these logs available to the operations team for monitoring and troubleshooting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational visibility into the nightly feed is required to detect and respond to data quality or availability issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall reject and quarantine individual recycled parts records from the ChoiceParts feed that fail data quality validation (e.g., missing mandatory fields, out-of-range values, unresolvable vehicle references), log each rejection with the reason, and continue processing the remaining valid records without aborting the synchronization job.
generated_requirements.rationale: BR-21 requires data validation; partial feed failures must not block the entire synchronization or degrade search quality.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a user satisfaction rating of 85% or higher across all user roles, and an ease-of-use rating of 95% or higher among Adjusters and Collision Estimators, as measured by post-go-live surveys administered within 30 days of launch.
generated_requirements.rationale: The BRD KPI table defines these satisfaction and ease-of-use targets as primary success criteria.
